fixer.summary: An empty line feed must precede any configured statement.
Braces must be placed as configured.
Class, trait and interface elements must be separated with one or none blank line.

source.code:
<?php
class Sample
{private $a; // foo
    /** second in a hour */
    private $b;
}

pint.output:
<?php
class Sample
{
private $a; // foo

    /** second in a hour */
    private $b;
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
class Sample
{private $a; // foo
    /** second in a hour */
    private $b;
}

fixer.summary: Renames PHPDoc tags.
Pre- or post-increment and decrement operators should be used if possible.

In method arguments and method call, there MUST NOT be a space before each comma and there MUST be one space after each comma. Argument lists MAY be split across multiple lines, where each subsequent line is indented once. When doing so, the first item in the list MUST be on the next line, and there MUST be only one argument per line.
fixer.description: This fixer covers rules defined in PSR2 ¶4.4, ¶4.6.

fixer.summary: Classes, constants, properties, and methods MUST have visibility declared, and keyword modifiers MUST be in the following order: inheritance modifier (`abstract` or `final`), visibility modifier (`public`, `protected`, or `private`), set-visibility modifier (`public(set)`, `protected(set)`, or `private(set)`), scope modifier (`static`), mutation modifier (`readonly`), type declaration, name.
Forbid multi-line whitespace before the closing semicolon or move the semicolon to the new line for chained calls.
All instances created with `new` keyword must (not) be followed by parentheses.
Removes extra blank lines and/or blank lines following configuration.

source.code:
<?php

switch ($foo) {
    case 41:
        echo "foo";
        break;

    case 42:
        break;
}

pint.output:
<?php

switch ($foo) {
    case 41:
        echo "foo";
        break;

    case 42:
        break;
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php

switch ($foo) {
    case 41:
        echo "foo";
        break;
    case 42:
        break;
}

fixer.summary: Removes `@param`, `@return` and `@var` tags that don't provide any useful information.

source.code:
<?php
class Foo {
    /**
     * @param Bar $bar
     * @param mixed $baz
     * @param string|int|null $qux
     * @param mixed $foo
     */
    public function doFoo(Bar $bar, $baz /*, $qux = null */) {}
}

pint.output:
<?php
class Foo {
    /**
     * @param mixed $baz
     * @param string|int|null $qux
     * @param mixed $foo
     */
    public function doFoo(Bar $bar, $baz /*, $qux = null */) {}
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
class Foo {
    /**
     * @param mixed $baz
     * @param string|int|null $qux
     */
    public function doFoo(Bar $bar, $baz /*, $qux = null */) {}
}

fixer.summary: Removes unneeded braces that are superfluous and aren't part of a control structure's body.
Removes unneeded parentheses around control statements.

source.selection_reason: Exercises `yield_from`, which is enabled in PHP-CS-Fixer's defaults but not in Pint's preset.

fixer.summary: In array declaration, there MUST NOT be a whitespace before each comma.
Ordering `use` statements.

source.code:
<?php
use const BBB;
use const AAAA;

use Acme;
use AAC;
use Bar;

use function DDD;
use function CCC\AA;

pint.output:
<?php
use const AAAA;
use const BBB;

use AAC;
use Acme;
use Bar;

use function CCC\AA;
use function DDD;

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
use AAC;
use Acme;

use Bar;
use function CCC\AA;
use function DDD;

use const AAAA;
use const BBB;

fixer.summary: Enforce camel (or snake) case for PHPUnit test methods, following configuration.

source.code:
<?php
class MyTest extends \PhpUnit\FrameWork\TestCase
{
    public function testMyCode() {}
}

pint.output:
<?php
class MyTest extends \PhpUnit\FrameWork\TestCase
{
    public function test_my_code() {}
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
class MyTest extends \PhpUnit\FrameWork\TestCase
{
    public function testMyCode() {}
}

fixer.summary: All items of the given PHPDoc tags must be either left-aligned or (by default) aligned vertically.

source.code:
<?php
/**
 * @param  EngineInterface $templating
 * @param string      $format
 * @param  int  $code       an HTTP response status code
 * @param    bool         $debug
 * @param  mixed    &$reference     a parameter passed by reference
 *
 * @return Foo description foo
 *
 * @throws Foo            description foo
 *             description foo
 *
 */

pint.output:
<?php
/**
 * @param  EngineInterface  $templating
 * @param  string  $format
 * @param  int  $code  an HTTP response status code
 * @param  bool  $debug
 * @param  mixed  &$reference  a parameter passed by reference
 *
 * @return Foo description foo
 *
 * @throws Foo description foo
 *             description foo
 *
 */

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
/**
 * @param EngineInterface $templating
 * @param string          $format
 * @param int             $code       an HTTP response status code
 * @param bool            $debug
 * @param mixed           &$reference a parameter passed by reference
 *
 * @return Foo description foo
 *
 * @throws Foo description foo
 *             description foo
 *
 */

fixer.summary: Scalar types should always be written in the same form. `int` not `integer`, `bool` not `boolean`, `float` not `real` or `double`.

source.selection_reason: Uses a scalar alias that PHP-CS-Fixer normalizes and Pint intentionally leaves alone.

fixer.summary: Annotations in PHPDoc should be grouped together so that annotations of the same type immediately follow each other. Annotations of a different type are separated by a single blank line.

source.code:
<?php
/**
 * Hello there!
 *
 * @author John Doe
 * @custom Test!
 *
 * @throws Exception|RuntimeException foo
 * @param string $foo
 *
 * @param bool   $bar Bar
 * @return int  Return the number of changes.
 */

pint.output:
<?php
/**
 * Hello there!
 *
 * @author John Doe
 *
 * @custom Test!
 *
 * @throws Exception|RuntimeException foo
 *
 * @param string $foo
 * @param bool   $bar Bar
 * @return int  Return the number of changes.
 */

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
/**
 * Hello there!
 *
 * @author John Doe
 *
 * @custom Test!
 *
 * @throws Exception|RuntimeException foo
 *
 * @param string $foo
 * @param bool   $bar Bar
 *
 * @return int  Return the number of changes.
 */

fixer.summary: Single-line comments and multi-line comments with only one line of actual content should use the `//` syntax.

source.code:
<?php
/* first comment */
$a = 1;

/*
 * second comment
 */
$b = 2;

/*
 * third
 * comment
 */
$c = 3;

pint.output:
<?php
/* first comment */
$a = 1;

/*
 * second comment
 */
$b = 2;

/*
 * third
 * comment
 */
$c = 3;

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
// first comment
$a = 1;

// second comment
$b = 2;

/*
 * third
 * comment
 */
$c = 3;

fixer.summary: Fix whitespace after a semicolon.
Each statement must be indented.

source.code:
<?php
        // foo
if ($foo) {
    echo "foo";
        // this is treated as comment of `if` block, as `stick_comment_to_next_continuous_control_statement` is disabled
} else {
    $aaa = 1;
}

pint.output:
<?php
// foo
if ($foo) {
    echo "foo";
    // this is treated as comment of `if` block, as `stick_comment_to_next_continuous_control_statement` is disabled
} else {
    $aaa = 1;
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
// foo
if ($foo) {
    echo "foo";
// this is treated as comment of `if` block, as `stick_comment_to_next_continuous_control_statement` is disabled
} else {
    $aaa = 1;
}

fixer.summary: Arguments lists, array destructuring lists, arrays that are multi-line, `match`-lines and parameters lists must have a trailing comma.
In array declaration, there MUST be a whitespace after each comma.
Write conditions in Yoda style (`true`), non-Yoda style (`['equal' => false, 'identical' => false, 'less_and_greater' => false]`) or ignore those conditions (`null`) based on configuration.

pint.output:
<?php
    // Enforce non-Yoda style.
    if ($a === null) {
        echo "null";
    }

fixer.summary: Each line of multi-line DocComments must have an asterisk [PSR-5] and must be aligned with the first one.

source.code:
<?php
    /**
            * This is a DOC Comment
with a line not prefixed with asterisk

   */

pint.output:
<?php
    /**
            * This is a DOC Comment
with a line not prefixed with asterisk

   */

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
    /**
     * This is a DOC Comment
     * with a line not prefixed with asterisk
     *
     */

fixer.summary: Converts simple usages of `array_push($x, $y);` to `$x[] = $y;`.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when the function `array_push` is overridden.

fixer.summary: Remove spaces before and after the attributes block.

source.code:
<?php
class User
{
    #[
        ApiProperty(identifier: true)
    ]
    private string $name;
}

pint.output:
<?php
class User
{
    #[
        ApiProperty(identifier: true)
    ]
    private string $name;
}

fixer.summary: Converts backtick operators to `shell_exec` calls.
fixer.description: Conversion is done only when it is non risky, so when special chars like single-quotes, double-quotes and backticks are not used inside the command.

fixer.summary: When referencing an internal class it must be written using the correct casing.
Using `isset($var) &&` multiple times should be done in one call.
Calling `unset` on multiple items should be done in one call.
Replace multiple nested calls of `dirname` by only one call with second `$level` parameter.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when the function `dirname` is overridden.

fixer.summary: Replaces `dirname(__FILE__)` expression with equivalent `__DIR__` constant.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when the function `dirname` is overridden.

fixer.summary: Replaces short-echo `<?=` with long format `<?php echo`/`<?php print` syntax, or vice-versa.

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php echo 1?>
<?php print '2' . '3'; ?>
<?php /* comment */ echo '2' . '3'; ?>
<?php print '2' . '3'; someFunction(); ?>

fixer.summary: Empty loop-body must be in configured style.
Empty loop-condition must be in configured style.

source.code:
<?php
for(;;) {
    foo();
}

do {
    foo();
} while(true); // do while

pint.output:
<?php
for(;;) {
    foo();
}

do {
    foo();
} while(true); // do while

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
while (true) {
    foo();
}

while (true) {
    foo();
}  // do while

fixer.summary: Replace deprecated `ereg` regular expression functions with `preg`.
fixer.risky_description: Risky if the `ereg` function is overridden.

fixer.summary: Error control operator should be added to deprecation notices and/or removed from other cases.
fixer.risky_description: Risky because adding/removing `@` might cause changes to code behaviour or if `trigger_error` function is overridden.

fixer.summary: Add curly braces to indirect variables to make them clear to understand.
Converts implicit variables into explicit ones in double-quoted strings or heredoc syntax.
fixer.description:
The reasoning behind this rule is the following:
- When there are two valid ways of doing the same thing, using both is confusing, there should be a coding standard to follow.
- PHP manual marks `"$var"` syntax as implicit and `"{$var}"` syntax as explicit: explicit code should always be preferred.
- Explicit syntax allows word concatenation inside strings, e.g. `"{$var}IsAVar"`, implicit doesn't.
- Explicit syntax is easier to detect for IDE/editors and therefore has colors/highlight with higher contrast, which is easier to read.
Backtick operator is skipped because it is harder to handle; you can use `backtick_to_shell_exec` fixer to normalize backticks to strings.

source.code:
<?php
$a = "My name is $name !";
$b = "I live in $state->country !";
$c = "I have $farm[0] chickens !";

pint.output:
<?php
$a = "My name is $name !";
$b = "I live in $state->country !";
$c = "I have $farm[0] chickens !";

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
$a = "My name is {$name} !";
$b = "I live in {$state->country} !";
$c = "I have {$farm[0]} chickens !";

fixer.summary: Order the flags in `fopen` calls, `b` and `t` must be last.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when the function `fopen` is overridden.

fixer.summary: The flags in `fopen` calls must omit `t`, and `b` must be omitted or included consistently.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when the function `fopen` is overridden.

fixer.summary: Replace core functions calls returning constants with the constants.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when any of the configured functions to replace are overridden.

source.code:
<?php
echo phpversion();
echo pi();
echo php_sapi_name();
class Foo
{
    public function Bar()
    {
        echo get_class();
        echo get_called_class();
    }
}

pint.output:
<?php
echo phpversion();
echo pi();
echo php_sapi_name();
class Foo
{
    public function Bar()
    {
        echo get_class();
        echo get_called_class();
    }
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
echo PHP_VERSION;
echo M_PI;
echo PHP_SAPI;
class Foo
{
    public function Bar()
    {
        echo self::class;
        echo static::class;
    }
}

fixer.summary: Replace `get_class` calls on object variables with class keyword syntax.
fixer.risky_description: Risky if the `get_class` function is overridden.

fixer.summary: Imports or fully qualifies global classes/functions/constants.

source.code:
<?php

namespace Foo;

use DateTimeImmutable;
use function count;
use const M_PI;

if (count($x)) {
    /** @var DateTimeImmutable $d */
    $d = new DateTimeImmutable();
    $p = M_PI;
}

pint.output:
<?php

namespace Foo;

use DateTimeImmutable;
use function count;
use const M_PI;

if (count($x)) {
    /** @var DateTimeImmutable $d */
    $d = new DateTimeImmutable();
    $p = M_PI;
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php

namespace Foo;

use DateTimeImmutable;
use function count;
use const M_PI;

if (\count($x)) {
    /** @var \DateTimeImmutable $d */
    $d = new \DateTimeImmutable();
    $p = \M_PI;
}

fixer.summary: Function `implode` must be called with 2 arguments in the documented order.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when the function `implode` is overridden.

fixer.summary: Replaces `is_null($var)` expression with `null === $var`.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when the function `is_null` is overridden.

fixer.summary: Use `&&` and `||` logical operators instead of `and` and `or`.
fixer.risky_description: Risky, because you must double-check if using and/or with lower precedence was intentional.

fixer.summary: Shorthand notation for operators should be used if possible.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when applying for string offsets (e.g. `<?php $text = "foo"; $text[0] = $text[0] & "\x7F";`).

fixer.summary: Use new serialization methods `__serialize` and `__unserialize` instead of deprecated ones `__sleep` and `__wakeup`.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when calling the old methods directly or having logic in the `__sleep` and `__wakeup` methods.

source.code:
<?php class Foo {
    public function __sleep() {}
    public function __wakeup() {}
}

pint.output:
<?php class Foo {
    public function __sleep() {}
    public function __wakeup() {}
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php class Foo {
    public function __serialize() {}
    public function __unserialize(array $data) {}
}

fixer.summary: Replace `strpos()` and `stripos()` calls with `str_starts_with()` or `str_contains()` if possible.
fixer.risky_description: Risky if `strpos`, `stripos`, `str_starts_with`, `str_contains` or `strtolower` functions are overridden.

source.code:
<?php
if (strpos($haystack, $needle) === 0) {}
if (strpos($haystack, $needle) !== 0) {}
if (strpos($haystack, $needle) !== false) {}
if (strpos($haystack, $needle) === false) {}

pint.output:
<?php
if (strpos($haystack, $needle) === 0) {}
if (strpos($haystack, $needle) !== 0) {}
if (strpos($haystack, $needle) !== false) {}
if (strpos($haystack, $needle) === false) {}

fixer.summary: Replaces `intval`, `floatval`, `doubleval`, `strval` and `boolval` function calls with according type casting operator.
fixer.risky_description: Risky if any of the functions `intval`, `floatval`, `doubleval`, `strval` or `boolval` are overridden.

fixer.summary: DocBlocks must start with two asterisks, multiline comments must start with a single asterisk, after the opening slash. Both must end with a single asterisk before the closing slash.

source.code:
<?php

/******
 * Multiline comment with arbitrary asterisks count
 ******/

/**\
 * Multiline comment that seems a DocBlock
 */

/**
 * DocBlock with arbitrary asterisk count at the end
 **/

pint.output:
<?php

/******
 * Multiline comment with arbitrary asterisks count
 ******/

/**\
 * Multiline comment that seems a DocBlock
 */

/**
 * DocBlock with arbitrary asterisk count at the end
 **/

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php

/*
 * Multiline comment with arbitrary asterisks count
 */

/*\
 * Multiline comment that seems a DocBlock
 */

/**
 * DocBlock with arbitrary asterisk count at the end
 */

fixer.summary: Add leading `\` before constant invocation of internal constant to speed up resolving. Constant name match is case-sensitive, except for `null`, `false` and `true`.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when any of the constants are namespaced or overridden.

fixer.summary: Add leading `\` before function invocation to speed up resolving.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when any of the functions are overridden.

source.code:
<?php
namespace space1 {
    echo count([1]);
}
namespace {
    echo count([1]);
}

pint.output:
<?php
namespace space1 {
    echo count([1]);
}
namespace {
    echo count([1]);
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
namespace space1 {
    echo \count([1]);
}
namespace {
    echo count([1]);
}

fixer.summary: There must be a comment when fall-through is intentional in a non-empty case body.
fixer.description: Adds a "no break" comment before fall-through cases, and removes it if there is no fall-through.

source.code:
<?php
switch ($foo) {
    case 1:
        foo();
    case 2:
        bar();
        // no break
        break;
    case 3:
        baz();
}

pint.output:
<?php
switch ($foo) {
    case 1:
        foo();
    case 2:
        bar();
        // no break
        break;
    case 3:
        baz();
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
switch ($foo) {
    case 1:
        foo();
        // no break
    case 2:
        bar();
        break;
    case 3:
        baz();
}

fixer.summary: There should not be any empty comments.
Replace accidental usage of homoglyphs (non ascii characters) in names.
fixer.risky_description: Renames classes and cannot rename the files. You might have string references to renamed code (`$$name`).

fixer.summary: Properties MUST not be explicitly initialised with `null` except when they have a type declaration (PHP 7.4).

source.code:
<?php
class Foo {
    public $bar = null;
    public ?string $baz = null;
    public ?string $baux;
}

pint.output:
<?php
class Foo {
    public $bar = null;
    public ?string $baz = null;
    public ?string $baux;
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
class Foo {
    public $bar;
    public ?string $baz = null;
    public ?string $baux;
}

fixer.summary: Convert PHP4-style constructors to `__construct`.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when old style constructor being fixed is overridden or overrides parent one.

fixer.summary: Replaces superfluous `elseif` with `if`.
Removes `final` from methods where possible.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when child class overrides a `private` method.

source.code:
<?php
final class Foo
{
    final public function foo1() {}
    final protected function bar() {}
    final private function baz() {}
}

class Bar
{
    final private function bar1() {}
}

pint.output:
<?php
final class Foo
{
    final public function foo1() {}
    final protected function bar() {}
    final private function baz() {}
}

class Bar
{
    final private function bar1() {}
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
final class Foo
{
    public function foo1() {}
    protected function bar() {}
    private function baz() {}
}

class Bar
{
    private function bar1() {}
}

fixer.summary: There should not be useless concat operations.
There should not be useless `else` cases.
There should not be useless Null-safe operator `?->` used.

source.code:
<?php
class Foo extends Bar
{
    public function test() {
        echo $this?->parentMethod();
    }
}

pint.output:
<?php
class Foo extends Bar
{
    public function test() {
        echo $this?->parentMethod();
    }
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
class Foo extends Bar
{
    public function test() {
        echo $this->parentMethod();
    }
}

fixer.summary: There must be no `sprintf` calls with only the first argument.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when if the `sprintf` function is overridden.

fixer.summary: Remove Zero-width space (ZWSP), Non-breaking space (NBSP) and other invisible unicode symbols.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when strings contain intended invisible characters.

fixer.summary: Operators - when multiline - must always be at the beginning or at the end of the line.
Orders the elements of classes/interfaces/traits/enums.
fixer.description:
Accepts a subset of pre-defined element types, special element groups, and custom patterns.

Element types: `['use_trait', 'public', 'protected', 'private', 'case', 'constant', 'constant_public', 'constant_protected', 'constant_private', 'property', 'property_static', 'property_public', 'property_protected', 'property_private', 'property_public_abstract', 'property_public_readonly', 'property_protected_abstract', 'property_protected_readonly', 'property_private_readonly', 'property_public_static', 'property_protected_static', 'property_private_static', 'method', 'method_abstract', 'method_static', 'method_public', 'method_protected', 'method_private', 'method_public_abstract', 'method_protected_abstract', 'method_private_abstract', 'method_public_abstract_static', 'method_protected_abstract_static', 'method_private_abstract_static', 'method_public_static', 'method_protected_static', 'method_private_static']`

Special element types: `['construct', 'destruct', 'magic', 'phpunit']`

Custom values:

- `method:*`: specify a single method name (e.g. `method:__invoke`) to set the order of that specific method.

source.code:
<?php
final class Example
{
    use BarTrait;
    use BazTrait;
    const C1 = 1;
    const C2 = 2;
    protected static $protStatProp;
    public static $pubStatProp1;
    public $pubProp1;
    protected $protProp;
    var $pubProp2;
    private static $privStatProp;
    private $privProp;
    public static $pubStatProp2;
    public $pubProp3;
    protected function __construct() {}
    private static function privStatFunc() {}
    public function pubFunc1() {}
    public function __toString() {}
    protected function protFunc() {}
    function pubFunc2() {}
    public static function pubStatFunc1() {}
    public function pubFunc3() {}
    static function pubStatFunc2() {}
    private function privFunc() {}
    public static function pubStatFunc3() {}
    protected static function protStatFunc() {}
    public function __destruct() {}
}

pint.output:
<?php
final class Example
{
    use BarTrait;
    use BazTrait;
    const C1 = 1;
    const C2 = 2;
    protected static $protStatProp;
    public static $pubStatProp1;
    public $pubProp1;
    protected $protProp;
    var $pubProp2;
    private static $privStatProp;
    private $privProp;
    public static $pubStatProp2;
    public $pubProp3;
    protected function __construct() {}
    private static function privStatFunc() {}
    public function pubFunc1() {}
    public function __toString() {}
    protected function protFunc() {}
    function pubFunc2() {}
    public static function pubStatFunc1() {}
    public function pubFunc3() {}
    static function pubStatFunc2() {}
    private function privFunc() {}
    public static function pubStatFunc3() {}
    protected static function protStatFunc() {}
    public function __destruct() {}
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
final class Example
{
    use BarTrait;
    use BazTrait;
    const C1 = 1;
    const C2 = 2;
    public static $pubStatProp1;
    public $pubProp1;
    var $pubProp2;
    public static $pubStatProp2;
    public $pubProp3;
    protected static $protStatProp;
    protected $protProp;
    private static $privStatProp;
    private $privProp;
    protected function __construct() {}
    public function __destruct() {}
    public function __toString() {}
    public function pubFunc1() {}
    function pubFunc2() {}
    public static function pubStatFunc1() {}
    public function pubFunc3() {}
    static function pubStatFunc2() {}
    public static function pubStatFunc3() {}
    protected function protFunc() {}
    protected static function protStatFunc() {}
    private static function privStatFunc() {}
    private function privFunc() {}
}

fixer.summary: Sort union types and intersection types using configured order.
PHPUnit assertion method calls like `->assertSame(true, $foo)` should be written with dedicated method like `->assertTrue($foo)`.
fixer.risky_description: Fixer could be risky if one is overriding PHPUnit's native methods.

source.code:
<?php
final class FooTest extends \PHPUnit_Framework_TestCase {
    public function testSomething() {
        $this->assertEquals(false, $b);
        $this->assertSame(true, $a);
        $this->assertNotEquals(null, $c);
        $this->assertNotSame(null, $d);
    }
}

pint.output:
<?php
final class FooTest extends \PHPUnit_Framework_TestCase {
    public function testSomething() {
        $this->assertEquals(false, $b);
        $this->assertSame(true, $a);
        $this->assertNotEquals(null, $c);
        $this->assertNotSame(null, $d);
    }
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
final class FooTest extends \PHPUnit_Framework_TestCase {
    public function testSomething() {
        $this->assertFalse($b);
        $this->assertTrue($a);
        $this->assertNotNull($c);
        $this->assertNotNull($d);
    }
}

fixer.summary: Data provider method must be placed after/before the last/first test where used.

source.code:
<?php
class FooTest extends TestCase {
    public function dataProvider() {}
    /**
     * @dataProvider dataProvider
     */
    public function testSomething($expected, $actual) {}
}

pint.output:
<?php
class FooTest extends TestCase {
    public function dataProvider() {}
    /**
     * @dataProvider dataProvider
     */
    public function testSomething($expected, $actual) {}
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
class FooTest extends TestCase {
    /**
     * @dataProvider dataProvider
     */
    public function testSomething($expected, $actual) {}
    public function dataProvider() {}
}

fixer.summary: PHPUnit annotations should be a FQCNs including a root namespace.

source.code:
<?php
final class MyTest extends \PHPUnit_Framework_TestCase
{
    /**
     * @expectedException InvalidArgumentException
     * @covers Project\NameSpace\Something
     * @coversDefaultClass Project\Default
     * @uses Project\Test\Util
     */
    public function testSomeTest()
    {
    }
}

pint.output:
<?php
final class MyTest extends \PHPUnit_Framework_TestCase
{
    /**
     * @expectedException InvalidArgumentException
     * @covers Project\NameSpace\Something
     * @coversDefaultClass Project\Default
     * @uses Project\Test\Util
     */
    public function testSomeTest()
    {
    }
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
final class MyTest extends \PHPUnit_Framework_TestCase
{
    /**
     * @expectedException \InvalidArgumentException
     * @covers \Project\NameSpace\Something
     * @coversDefaultClass \Project\Default
     * @uses \Project\Test\Util
     */
    public function testSomeTest()
    {
    }
}

fixer.summary: All PHPUnit test classes should be marked as internal.
Usage of PHPUnit's mock e.g. `->will($this->returnValue(..))` must be replaced by its shorter equivalent such as `->willReturn(...)`.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when PHPUnit classes are overridden or not accessible, or when project has PHPUnit incompatibilities.

source.code:
<?php
final class MyTest extends \PHPUnit_Framework_TestCase
{
    public function testSomeTest()
    {
        $someMock = $this->createMock(Some::class);
        $someMock->method("some")->will($this->returnSelf());
        $someMock->method("some")->will($this->returnValue("example"));
        $someMock->method("some")->will($this->returnArgument(2));
        $someMock->method("some")->will($this->returnCallback("str_rot13"));
        $someMock->method("some")->will($this->returnValueMap(["a","b","c"]));
    }
}

pint.output:
<?php
final class MyTest extends \PHPUnit_Framework_TestCase
{
    public function testSomeTest()
    {
        $someMock = $this->createMock(Some::class);
        $someMock->method("some")->will($this->returnSelf());
        $someMock->method("some")->will($this->returnValue("example"));
        $someMock->method("some")->will($this->returnArgument(2));
        $someMock->method("some")->will($this->returnCallback("str_rot13"));
        $someMock->method("some")->will($this->returnValueMap(["a","b","c"]));
    }
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
final class MyTest extends \PHPUnit_Framework_TestCase
{
    public function testSomeTest()
    {
        $someMock = $this->createMock(Some::class);
        $someMock->method("some")->willReturnSelf();
        $someMock->method("some")->willReturn("example");
        $someMock->method("some")->willReturnArgument(2);
        $someMock->method("some")->willReturnCallback("str_rot13");
        $someMock->method("some")->willReturnMap(["a","b","c"]);
    }
}

fixer.summary: Adds or removes @test annotations from tests, following configuration.
fixer.risky_description: This fixer may change the name of your tests, and could cause incompatibility with abstract classes or interfaces.

source.code:
<?php
class Test extends \PhpUnit\FrameWork\TestCase
{
    /**
     * @test
     */
    public function itDoesSomething() {} }

pint.output:
<?php
class Test extends \PhpUnit\FrameWork\TestCase
{
    /**
     * @test
     */
    public function itDoesSomething() {} }

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
class Test extends \PhpUnit\FrameWork\TestCase
{
    /**
     *
     */
    public function testItDoesSomething() {} }

fixer.summary: Adds a default `@coversNothing` annotation to PHPUnit test classes that have no `@covers*` annotation.

source.code:
<?php
final class MyTest extends \PHPUnit_Framework_TestCase
{
    public function testSomeTest()
    {
        $this->assertSame(a(), b());
    }
}

pint.output:
<?php
final class MyTest extends \PHPUnit_Framework_TestCase
{
    public function testSomeTest()
    {
        $this->assertSame(a(), b());
    }
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php

/**
 * @coversNothing
 */
final class MyTest extends \PHPUnit_Framework_TestCase
{
    public function testSomeTest()
    {
        $this->assertSame(a(), b());
    }
}

fixer.summary: PHPDoc should contain `@param` for all params.
PHPDoc annotation descriptions should not be a sentence.
No alias PHPDoc tags should be used.

source.code:
<?php
/**
 * @property string $foo
 * @property-read string $bar
 *
 * @link baz
 */
final class Example
{
}

pint.output:
<?php
/**
 * @property string $foo
 * @property-read string $bar
 *
 * @link baz
 */
final class Example
{
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
/**
 * @property string $foo
 * @property string $bar
 *
 * @see baz
 */
final class Example
{
}

fixer.summary: `@return void` and `@return null` annotations must be removed from PHPDoc.
Order PHPDoc tags by value.

source.code:
<?php
/**
 * @covers Foo
 * @covers Bar
 */
final class MyTest extends \PHPUnit_Framework_TestCase
{}

pint.output:
<?php
/**
 * @covers Foo
 * @covers Bar
 */
final class MyTest extends \PHPUnit_Framework_TestCase
{}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
/**
 * @covers Bar
 * @covers Foo
 */
final class MyTest extends \PHPUnit_Framework_TestCase
{}

fixer.summary: The type of `@return` annotations of methods returning a reference to itself must the configured one.

source.code:
<?php
class Sample
{
    /**
     * @return this
     */
    public function test1()
    {
        return $this;
    }

    /**
     * @return $self
     */
    public function test2()
    {
        return $this;
    }
}

pint.output:
<?php
class Sample
{
    /**
     * @return this
     */
    public function test1()
    {
        return $this;
    }

    /**
     * @return $self
     */
    public function test2()
    {
        return $this;
    }
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
class Sample
{
    /**
     * @return $this
     */
    public function test1()
    {
        return $this;
    }

    /**
     * @return self
     */
    public function test2()
    {
        return $this;
    }
}

fixer.summary: PHPDoc summary should end in either a full stop, exclamation mark, or question mark.

source.code:
<?php
/**
 * Foo function is great
 */
function foo () {}

pint.output:
<?php
/**
 * Foo function is great
 */
function foo () {}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
/**
 * Foo function is great.
 */
function foo () {}

fixer.summary: Docblocks should only be used on structural elements.

source.code:
<?php
$first = true;// needed because by default first docblock is never fixed.

/** This should be a comment */
foreach($connections as $key => $sqlite) {
    $sqlite->open($path);
}

pint.output:
<?php
$first = true;// needed because by default first docblock is never fixed.

/** This should be a comment */
foreach($connections as $key => $sqlite) {
    $sqlite->open($path);
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
$first = true;// needed because by default first docblock is never fixed.

/* This should be a comment */
foreach($connections as $key => $sqlite) {
    $sqlite->open($path);
}

fixer.summary: Removes extra blank lines after summary and after description in PHPDoc.

source.code:
<?php
/**
 * Summary.
 *
 *
 * Description that contain 4 lines,
 *
 *
 * while 2 of them are blank!
 *
 *
 * @param string $foo
 *
 *
 * @dataProvider provideFixCases
 */
function fnc($foo) {}

pint.output:
<?php
/**
 * Summary.
 *
 *
 * Description that contain 4 lines,
 *
 *
 * while 2 of them are blank!
 *
 *
 * @param string $foo
 *
 *
 * @dataProvider provideFixCases
 */
function fnc($foo) {}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
/**
 * Summary.
 *
 * Description that contain 4 lines,
 *
 *
 * while 2 of them are blank!
 *
 * @param string $foo
 *
 * @dataProvider provideFixCases
 */
function fnc($foo) {}

fixer.summary: Removes duplicate PHPDoc types.
Sorts PHPDoc types.
`@var` and `@type` annotations must have type and name in the correct order.
Converts `pow` to the `**` operator.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when the function `pow` is overridden.

fixer.summary: Converts `protected` variables and methods to `private` where possible.

source.code:
<?php
final class Sample
{
    protected $a;

    protected function test()
    {
    }
}

pint.output:
<?php
final class Sample
{
    protected $a;

    protected function test()
    {
    }
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
final class Sample
{
    private $a;

    private function test()
    {
    }
}

fixer.summary: Classes must be in a path that matches their namespace, be at least one namespace deep and the class name should match the file name.
fixer.risky_description: This fixer may change your class name, which will break the code that depends on the old name.

source.selection_reason: This fixer depends on the file path, so the override supplies a stable path that makes the class-name correction visible.

fixer.summary: Local, dynamic and directly referenced variables should not be assigned and directly returned by a function or method.
Inside class or interface element `self` should be preferred to the class name itself.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when using dynamic calls like get_called_class() or late static binding.

source.code:
<?php
class Sample
{
    const BAZ = 1;
    const BAR = Sample::BAZ;

    public function getBar()
    {
        return Sample::BAR;
    }
}

pint.output:
<?php
class Sample
{
    const BAZ = 1;
    const BAR = Sample::BAZ;

    public function getBar()
    {
        return Sample::BAR;
    }
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
class Sample
{
    const BAZ = 1;
    const BAR = self::BAZ;

    public function getBar()
    {
        return self::BAR;
    }
}

fixer.summary: Instructions must be terminated with a semicolon.
Cast shall be used, not `settype`.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when the `settype` function is overridden or when used as the 2nd or 3rd expression in a `for` loop .

fixer.summary: Converts explicit variables in double-quoted strings and heredoc syntax from simple to complex format (`${` to `{$`).
fixer.description: Doesn't touch implicit variables. Works together nicely with `explicit_string_variable`.

fixer.summary: Each trait `use` must be done as single statement.

source.code:
<?php
final class Example
{
    use Foo, Bar;
}

pint.output:
<?php
final class Example
{
    use Foo, Bar;
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
final class Example
{
    use Foo;use Bar;
}

fixer.summary: Increment and decrement operators should be used if possible.
Lambdas not (indirectly) referencing `$this` must be declared `static`.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when using `->bindTo` on lambdas without referencing to `$this`.

fixer.summary: Handles implicit backslashes in strings and heredocs. Depending on the chosen strategy, it can escape implicit backslashes to ease the understanding of which are special chars interpreted by PHP and which not (`escape`), or it can remove these additional backslashes if you find them superfluous (`unescape`). You can also leave them as-is using `ignore` strategy.
fixer.description:
In PHP double-quoted strings and heredocs some chars like `n`, `$` or `u` have special meanings if preceded by a backslash (and some are special only if followed by other special chars), while a backslash preceding other chars are interpreted like a plain backslash. The precise list of those special chars is hard to remember and to identify quickly: this fixer escapes backslashes that do not start a special interpretation with the char after them.
It is possible to fix also single-quoted strings: in this case there is no special chars apart from single-quote and backslash itself, so the fixer simply ensure that all backslashes are escaped. Both single and double backslashes are allowed in single-quoted strings, so the purpose in this context is mainly to have a uniformed way to have them written all over the codebase.

source.code:
<?php

$singleQuoted = 'String with \" and My\Prefix\\';

$doubleQuoted = "Interpret my \n but not my \a";

$hereDoc = <<<HEREDOC
Interpret my \100 but not my \999
HEREDOC;

pint.output:
<?php

$singleQuoted = 'String with \" and My\Prefix\\';

$doubleQuoted = "Interpret my \n but not my \a";

$hereDoc = <<<HEREDOC
Interpret my \100 but not my \999
HEREDOC;

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php

$singleQuoted = 'String with \" and My\Prefix\\';

$doubleQuoted = "Interpret my \n but not my \\a";

$hereDoc = <<<HEREDOC
Interpret my \100 but not my \\999
HEREDOC;

fixer.summary: String tests for empty must be done against `''`, not with `strlen`.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when `strlen` is overridden, when called using a `stringable` object, also no longer triggers warning when called using non-string(able).

fixer.summary: All multi-line strings must use correct line ending.
fixer.risky_description: Changing the line endings of multi-line strings might affect string comparisons and outputs.

fixer.summary: Switch case must not be ended with `continue` but with `break`.
Use the Elvis operator `?:` where possible.
fixer.risky_description: Risky when relying on functions called on both sides of the `?` operator.

fixer.summary: Use `null` coalescing operator `??` where possible.

skipped.reason: The current Pint and PHP-CS-Fixer parameters normalize to the same output because `single_line` is enabled on both sides, so the remaining parameter differences are not observable in a standalone quiz sample.
No evaluated sample produced different Pint and PHP-CS-Fixer outputs for this rule.
